strategy: He opens by throwing two -50% Smoke Screens on each wizard, wrecking your accuracy.
Attack him once with a single-target spell to strip your Smoke Screens and earn a Mark of Courage.
That Mark (a Stun Shield) makes you immune to his round-5 Efreet (895 Fire plus -90% Weakness), so grab it before round 5. It survives Earthquake and cannot be stolen.
Hit with Fire or Myth (+30% incoming); avoid Ice (80% resist).
Blade or Feint, then burst the 4435 health with one clean hit.
cheatNotes: At the start and every 5 rounds he lays two -50% Smoke Screens on each wizard, tanking your accuracy.
Quench dispels the Smoke Screen if you would rather cleanse than attack through it.
Every fifth round he Efreets (895 Fire plus -90% Weakness) any wizard without a Mark of Courage, plus a free Earthquake. Have your Mark up.
Quench dispels the Efreet even though it shows no animation.
You need one Quench per Efreet you want to dispel.
Attacking him with a single-target spell clears your Smoke Screens and grants a Mark of Courage (a Stun Shield). Do this early to dodge the Efreet.
Pet may-casts will not earn the Mark, so make the attack yourself.
During the Mark-of-Courage round itself, direct attacks grant no Mark. Get yours in an earlier round.
The Mark is a Stun Shield, so Earthquake and Thieving Dragon cannot remove it. One direct hit early keeps you safe from the Efreet.
